behaviors.behavior: Incremental parse is byte-identical to parseSessionJsonl at every line-by-line growth step (production shape: content ends on \n)
behaviors.evidence: incremental-session-parse.test.ts 'matches parseSessionJsonl at every line-by-line growth step' PASSED

behaviors.behavior: Byte-identical at every CHAR-by-char growth step (exercises the trailing partial line: mid-JSON and mid-surrogate tails)
behaviors.evidence: incremental-session-parse.test.ts 'matches ... at every CHAR-by-char growth step' PASSED; adversarial subagent ran ~500 random corpora char-by-char (LF+CRLF, escape/surrogate/raw-newline-in-value) deep-equal to batch

behaviors.behavior: No-trailing-newline content and the empty string parse identically to batch
behaviors.evidence: incremental-session-parse.test.ts 'NO trailing newline' + 'the empty string' PASSED

behaviors.behavior: Already-committed event objects keep referential identity across an append (only new events are new objects)
behaviors.evidence: incremental-session-parse.test.ts 'keeps already-parsed event objects referentially identical across an append' PASSED

behaviors.behavior: Advancing does not mutate the previous state's arrays
behaviors.evidence: incremental-session-parse.test.ts 'does NOT mutate the previous state's arrays' PASSED

behaviors.behavior: A shrunk content (truncation) triggers a full re-parse equal to batch
behaviors.evidence: incremental-session-parse.test.ts 'full-reparses when content shrinks' PASSED

behaviors.behavior: A differing prefix (rotation/replacement) triggers a full re-parse equal to batch
behaviors.evidence: incremental-session-parse.test.ts 'full-reparses when the prefix differs' PASSED

behaviors.behavior: rotation to empty then re-grow parses correctly
behaviors.evidence: incremental-session-parse.test.ts 'resets cleanly to empty then re-grows' PASSED

behaviors.behavior: An idle poll (unchanged content) returns the SAME state and result reference (no downstream memo churn)
behaviors.evidence: incremental-session-parse.test.ts 'returns the SAME state (and result) object when content is unchanged' PASSED

behaviors.behavior: Malformed-line accounting matches batch (unknown stub for a middle line, swallowed torn tail, malformedLines equal)
behaviors.evidence: incremental-session-parse.test.ts 'preserves the batch parser's unknown-stub + malformedLines behaviour' PASSED

behaviors.behavior: An append hands parseSessionJsonl only the appended region (< 1/10 of the accumulated string); an idle poll calls it zero times (the actual optimization)
behaviors.evidence: incremental-session-parse.mechanism.test.ts 2 cases PASSED (parseSessionJsonl spied through)

behaviors.behavior: The transcript surfaces render in a real browser with the incremental parser (mode/pr-link/stop-hook + custom-title/agent-name/permission-mode chips, no Unknown card)
behaviors.evidence: F0.5 web: e2e 90-transcript-renderer-fingerprints + 59-parser-variants, 2/2 passed on Chromium against the isolated real Hono stack, exit 0

surface_verification.justification: Behaviour-preserving client renderer change (Spec Impact NONE): the transcript pane now parses only the appended byte-delta instead of re-parsing the whole accumulated JSONL each poll, and memoizes the markdown leaf. The rendered bubbles are byte-identical, so a real browser against a real server is the honest way to show nothing regressed. Isolated single-process stack: temp USERPROFILE, built client served via SHIPWRIGHT_STATIC_DIR, Hono on 127.0.0.1:3848, SHIPWRIGHT_NETWORK_PROFILE=local; the live WebUI on :3847 was never touched. 90-transcript-renderer-fingerprints (mode/pr-link/stop-hook surfaces, no Unknown card) + 59-parser-variants (custom-title/agent-name/permission-mode chips) both passed 2/2 on Chromium, exit 0.
